Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Embracing Bayesian methods in clinical trials 68 upvotes, 6 comments
  2. Can It Resolve DOOM? Game Engine in 2k DNS Records 37 upvotes, 2 comments
  3. Explore the Hidden World of Sand 156 upvotes, 33 comments
  4. ISBN Visualization – Annas Archive 55 upvotes, 9 comments
  5. Meow.camera 160 upvotes, 37 comments
  6. Nashville library launches Memory Lab for digitizing home movies 73 upvotes, 14 comments
  7. Building FireStriker: Making Civic Tech Free 80 upvotes, 17 comments
  8. Installing a Let's Encrypt TLS certificate on a Brother printer with Certbot 172 upvotes, 45 comments
  9. Capability-Based Security for Redox: Namespace and CWD as Capabilities 15 upvotes, 1 comments
  10. LG's new 1Hz display is the secret behind a new laptop's battery life 65 upvotes, 18 comments
  11. Velxio 2.0 – Emulate Arduino, ESP32, and Raspberry Pi 3 in the Browser 44 upvotes, 11 comments
  12. Solving Semantle with the Wrong Embeddings 8 upvotes, 0 comments
  13. Desk for people who work at home with a cat 305 upvotes, 121 comments
  14. DOJ confirms FBI Director Kash Patel's personal email was hacked 56 upvotes, 16 comments
  15. Gzip decompression in 250 lines of Rust 105 upvotes, 38 comments
  16. If you don't opt out by Apr 24 GitHub will train on your private repos 513 upvotes, 241 comments
  17. Anatomy of the .claude/ folder 334 upvotes, 173 comments
  18. Make macOS consistently bad (unironically) 222 upvotes, 154 comments
  19. Everything old is new again: memory optimization 165 upvotes, 116 comments
  20. Hold on to Your Hardware 545 upvotes, 441 comments
  21. People inside Microsoft are fighting to drop mandatory Microsoft Account 450 upvotes, 365 comments
  22. The 'paperwork flood': How I drowned a bureaucrat before dinner 505 upvotes, 413 comments
  23. EMachines never obsolete PCs: More than a meme 57 upvotes, 36 comments
  24. The Future of SCIP 15 upvotes, 6 comments
  25. ‘Energy independence feels practical’: Europeans building mini solar farms 186 upvotes, 163 comments
  26. Slovenia becomes first EU country to introduce fuel rationing 92 upvotes, 113 comments
  27. Telnyx package compromised on PyPI 65 upvotes, 76 comments
  28. 21,864 Yugoslavian .yu domains 68 upvotes, 86 comments
  29. Automatically generate all 3D print files for organizing a drawer 10 upvotes, 7 comments
  30. Should QA exist? 81 upvotes, 120 comments

Last generated 7:00am, 28 March 2026. Source here.
